message:
fix(browser-core): remove missing sourcemap artifacts from manifest, offload wasm builds via rch

Package manifest cleanup:
- Remove asupersync.js.map and asupersync_bg.wasm.map from both the
  "exports" map and "files" array in packages/browser-core/package.json.
  These sourcemap files are only generated during debug builds and were
  never present on disk for release builds—causing npm pack to silently
  produce incomplete tarballs and consumers to get 404s when devtools
  tried to fetch them.
- Also remove README.md from the files array since no README exists in
  the browser-core package directory.

Build script rch offloading:
- build_browser_core_artifacts.sh now requires rch (remote compilation
  helper) and wraps wasm-pack's cargo invocations through a generated
  cargo-rch shim script. This offloads the actual cargo build to the rch
  fleet, keeping the local machine free for other agent work during the
  ~2-3 minute wasm-opt pass.

Validation and contract tests:
- validate_npm_pack_smoke.sh: add a Phase 3 check that iterates over
  the files array entries and verifies each exists on disk, catching
  manifest/reality drift before publish.
- artifact_layout.rs: split the sourcemap export assertions into a
  separate test (browser_core_manifest_avoids_advertising_optional_or_
  missing_artifacts) that asserts both exports and files do NOT contain
  the missing artifacts.
- wasm_npm_pack_smoke_contract.rs: new test
  browser_core_files_array_only_advertises_existing_artifacts that
  asserts every files[] entry exists on disk.
- wasm_rust_browser_example_contract.rs: new test
  browser_core_build_script_exists_and_offloads_wasm_builds_via_rch
  that asserts the build script contains the rch wrapper markers.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -11,6 +11,8 @@ PACKAGE_DIR="${REPO_ROOT}/packages/browser-core"
 ABI_FILE="${REPO_ROOT}/src/types/wasm_abi.rs"
 JS_MAP_FILE="asupersync.js.map"
 WASM_MAP_FILE="asupersync_bg.wasm.map"
+RCH_BIN="${RCH_BIN:-rch}"
+WRAPPER_ROOT="${REPO_ROOT}/target/browser-core-build"
 
 if ! command -v wasm-pack >/dev/null 2>&1; then
   echo "error: wasm-pack is required (https://rustwasm.github.io/wasm-pack/)" >&2
@@ -22,6 +24,11 @@ if ! command -v rg >/dev/null 2>&1; then
   exit 1
 fi
 
+if ! command -v "${RCH_BIN}" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
+  echo "error: ${RCH_BIN} is required to offload cargo-backed wasm builds" >&2
+  exit 1
+fi
+
 case "${PROFILE}" in
   minimal)
     BUILD_ARGS=(--release -- --no-default-features --features minimal)
@@ -44,10 +51,20 @@ USAGE
     ;;
 esac
 
-mkdir -p "${STAGING_DIR}" "${PACKAGE_DIR}"
+mkdir -p "${STAGING_DIR}" "${PACKAGE_DIR}" "${WRAPPER_ROOT}"
+WORK_DIR="$(mktemp -d "${WRAPPER_ROOT}/${PROFILE}.XXXXXX")"
+CARGO_WRAPPER="${WORK_DIR}/cargo-rch"
+
+cat > "${CARGO_WRAPPER}" <<EOF
+#!/usr/bin/env bash
+set -euo pipefail
+cd "${REPO_ROOT}"
+exec "${RCH_BIN}" exec -- cargo "\$@"
+EOF
+chmod +x "${CARGO_WRAPPER}"
 
 echo "==> Building asupersync-browser-core (${PROFILE})"
-wasm-pack build "${CRATE_DIR}" \
+CARGO="${CARGO_WRAPPER}" wasm-pack build "${CRATE_DIR}" \
   --target web \
   --out-dir "${STAGING_DIR}" \
   --out-name asupersync \

@@ -241,6 +241,15 @@ for entry in "${ARTIFACTS[@]}"; do
     warn "artifact not built yet: ${artifact} (run build:wasm first)"
   fi
 done
+
+while IFS= read -r rel; do
+  [[ -n "$rel" ]] || continue
+  if [[ -e "${BC}/${rel}" ]]; then
+    ok "files array artifact exists on disk: ${rel}"
+  else
+    err "files array advertises missing artifact: ${rel}"
+  fi
+done <<< "$bc_files"
 echo ""
 
 # ── Phase 4: Higher-Level Package Source ──────────────────────────────
